title: Performance Benchmarking
description: Performance engineer runs JMH micro-benchmarks for core CDC pipeline components

actors.name: PerformanceEngineer
actors.description: Triggers and observes JMH benchmark execution to assess throughput/latency of pipeline components

actors.name: JMH_Runner
actors.description: JMH harness that orchestrates @Setup, @Benchmark, @TearDown lifecycle, warms up JVM, and collects metrics

use_cases.name: Benchmark ChangeEventQueue Producer Throughput
use_cases.description: Measures enqueue() throughput while a background consumer thread continuously polls the queue
use_cases.setup: Build ChangeEventQueue with DefaultQueueProvider; start consumer thread

use_cases.name: Benchmark ChangeEventQueue Consumer Throughput
use_cases.description: Measures poll() throughput while a background producer thread continuously enqueues events

use_cases.name: Benchmark Full Queue Cycle (10M records, 1 producer)
use_cases.description: Measures average time to enqueue+consume 10,000,000 DataChangeEvents end-to-end with 1 producer and 1 consumer

use_cases.name: Benchmark Multi-Writer Queue (10 producers x 1M records)
use_cases.description: Measures average time for 10 concurrent producers (1M records each = 10M total) and 1 consumer
use_cases.notes: Tests contention under concurrent write pressure

use_cases.name: Benchmark SMT NewRecord Overhead
use_cases.description: Measures throughput of a SMT that copies a SourceRecord via record.newRecord()

use_cases.name: Benchmark SMT NoOp Overhead
use_cases.description: Baseline: measures throughput of a no-op SMT that returns the same record unchanged

use_cases.name: Benchmark SMT Base (empty method)
use_cases.description: Empty benchmark method; pure JMH overhead baseline for SMT comparisons

use_cases.name: Benchmark IncrementalSnapshotContext Serialization
use_cases.description: Measures throughput of serializing incremental snapshot context (offset map) as table count scales
use_cases.setup: Generate N^2 table IDs (db_i.table_j), add with AdditionalCondition filter 'color=blue'

use_cases.name: Benchmark TableId Parsing
use_cases.description: Measures average time for TableId.parse() across simple and quoted identifier forms

use_cases.name: Benchmark MySQL DDL Parser (ANTLR)
use_cases.description: Measures average parsing time for CREATE TABLE DDL statements as column count grows, using ANTLR-based parser
use_cases.setup: Build 'CREATE TABLE t1 (id int PK, v0..vN int)' statement; instantiate MySqlAntlrDdlParser + Tables

key_relationships: PerformanceEngineer initiates all 10 use cases via JMH CLI
JMH_Runner manages @Setup/@TearDown lifecycle for each benchmark class
ChangeEventQueuePerf.ConsumerPerf includes ChangeEventQueue + background producer thread
ChangeEventQueuePerf.QueuePerf and MultiWriterQueuePerf extend to end-to-end throughput measurement
SmtOverheadPerf contains NewRecord and NoOp inner classes both implementing Transformation<SourceRecord> + Versioned
SmtOverheadPerf depends_on Module.version() for Versioned interface
MySqlDdlParserPerf depends_on MySqlAntlrDdlParser extends AbstractDdlParser, Tables
important_notes: ChangeEventQueue benchmarks parameterize pollIntervalMillis to study back-pressure behavior
SMT benchmarks run at microsecond resolution (high-frequency ops)
TableId parsing benchmarks run at nanosecond resolution
IncrementalSnapshotContext benchmarks study O(N^2) table scaling behavior
MySqlDdlParserPerf measures ANTLR parser scaling with column count — legacy parser no longer present
SmtOverheadPerf.NewRecord and NoOp are private inner classes used only for benchmarking
